course_title: Advanced Chinese to English Translation in the Age of AI

learning_outcomes.description: translate more advanced text genres from Chinese into English in a linguistically and culturally appropriate mannner
appreciate linguistic and cultural differences across more complex text genres
be aware of the key cultural and ethical issues in translation, as set out by NAATI
access and understand the application of translation resources
have a well-rounded linguistic vocabulary to talk about texts

Due every Wednesday from WEEK 3 to WEEK 10 in class in the first 15 minutes.

IMPORTANT: WEEK 3 - Wednesday is a public holiday. The in-class quiz will be conducted in a make-up session on Thursday.

Best 5 (out of 8) in-class quiz results for each student will be used to calculate the final mark for this assessment item.
assessments.task_description:
Type: In-class quizzes

Reading: 0 minutes

Duration: 15 minutes

Format: Translation or Translation Revision

An in-class quiz involving the translation of a short paragraph (not more than four sentences) or a translation revision task will be conducted each week from WEEKS 3-10. In WEEK 2 there will be a practice exercise designed to familiarise students with this task and this will not be calculated into the quiz total mark.

A hard copy of the quiz sheet will be delivered in class during the first 15 minutes of each session. Minor errors (like minor errors in spelling or capitalisation) will not be penalised. When the average is calculated at the end of the semester to finalise the score for this assessment item, only the 5 highest scores from WEEK 3 to WEEK 10 will be included. This means you may be able to achieve a full score for this assessment item even if you miss some of the exercises during the semester.

Paper dictionaries are allowed for the in-class quiz.

The best 5 (out of 8) in-class quiz results for each student will be used to calculate the final mark for this assessment item.

AI and MT Statement

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students submit completed translation exercise worksheets during class, immediately after the time provided.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
In-Class Oral Presentation (35%)

Timing: Weeks 11–12

Format: 5-minute timed presentation supported by one PowerPoint slide + palm cards

Prepare a presentation reflecting on a given translation task and deliver it in class. Students will have at least THREE weeks to prepare the presentation in advance. The presentation should critically analyse the translation process and demonstrate students' understanding of the source text as well as challenges and strategies involved in the translation process. During the presentation, students are NOT permitted to read from a prepared transcript; however, students may refer to palm cards occasionally to support their delivery.

The presentation should address the following areas:

  • An accurate understanding of the Source Text (ST): Provide a brief overview of the source text and discuss its key linguistic, cultural, stylistic, or contextual features.
  • Easy and difficult aspects of the translation task: Identify elements that were straightforward to translate and explain words/sentences that make the translation work challenging.
  • Translation strategies used: Discuss the translation methods and strategies (including any AI tool) you have applied to address specific issues and achieve an effective translation.
  • Reflection: Reflect on what you have learned from completing the translation task, including any skills, insights, technology or knowledge gained from the translation practice.

Presentation: 30% + Peer Assessment: 5%

Students will be assessed on the clarity of their analysis, the relevance and effectiveness of the translation strategies discussed, the depth of their reflection, and their ability to communicate their ideas effectively within the allocated time limit.

AI and MT Statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Presentation to be delivered in-person during class time. PPT to be submitted via Blackboard link on the day of the presentation.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam consists of multiple choice questions and a text for translation revision, testing students' knowledge and skills developed from all the weeks. The text for translation revision will represent the different genres studied in this course. Students may have access to lecture notes and paper dictionaries during the exam. 

The exam is online on Blackboard (Ultra). Be sure to access a reliable internet connection. You will have 60 minutes of working time plus 10 minutes for planning. Your time begins once you start the exam, and it must be completed in ONE attempt.

You may use any printed materials or online tools to complete this assessment, but you must demonstrate your independent thinking and understanding of translation and getting help from other people is strictly prohibited. Auto submission is set in the system.

AI and MT Statement

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment task is conducted on Blackboard (Ultra) and answers are automatically submitted at the designated end time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
